evidence: Android subscription purchase request sends SKU data but no subscription offer token or base-plan offer token. The installed IAP module expects offer tokens for subscription purchases.
policyRisk: High: Android users may see a subscription CTA but cannot complete the required Play Billing purchase flow.
technicalRisk: High: purchase can fail before a Play purchase is created; production subscription enablement is blocked.
recommendedFix: Fetch Play subscription offer details and call purchase with subscriptionOffers including sku and offerToken for the selected base plan or offer.
testsToAdd: Android unit/integration test that purchase request includes offerToken for monthly and yearly products; Play sandbox purchase test.

evidence: iOS client sends signed transaction data and finishes after backend verification, but source cannot prove StoreKit capability, App Store products, subscription group, or sandbox setup are active.
policyRisk: Medium: review may reject if IAP products are unavailable or misconfigured.
technicalRisk: Medium: purchase UI can be visible while store products are not ready.
recommendedFix: Verify EAS/App Store build capability and App Store Connect products; keep purchase UI disabled until sandbox purchase and restore pass.
testsToAdd: iOS sandbox tests for purchase, restore, renewal, expiration, refund, and revoke.

evidence: Stripe-managed users in the mobile subscription screen can open a Stripe customer portal URL in an external browser.
policyRisk: High: iOS and Android store review may treat this as steering to external subscription management or payment changes for digital features.
technicalRisk: Medium: users can encounter multiple billing systems from mobile, causing source confusion.
recommendedFix: Hide Stripe portal access in iOS/Android store builds; show web-only management instructions without an external payment link unless counsel confirms an exception.
testsToAdd: Mobile UI tests proving iOS and Android do not open Stripe portal or checkout for digital subscriptions.

evidence: Upgrade accessibility hint still says the action opens secure checkout in a browser even though mobile code should use IAP.
policyRisk: Medium: browser checkout language can be interpreted as external purchase steering or unfinished billing copy.
technicalRisk: Low: assistive technology users receive inaccurate purchase flow information.
recommendedFix: Change mobile billing copy to reference the native store purchase flow and remove browser-checkout wording from store builds.
testsToAdd: Accessibility snapshot test for upgrade CTA copy on iOS and Android.

evidence: Stripe checkout route authenticates and validates plan state but has no explicit server-side rejection for iOS or Android mobile clients.
policyRisk: High if a mobile client bug or direct call exposes external checkout for digital app features.
technicalRisk: Medium: authenticated mobile users could technically initiate web Stripe checkout.
recommendedFix: Add server-side web-only guard using platform/client headers and origin policy; block checkout for store-distributed mobile clients.
testsToAdd: API tests that iOS and Android client headers are rejected while web checkout still works.

evidence: Stripe portal route can create a portal session for any authenticated user with a Stripe customer ID and has no mobile-platform guard.
policyRisk: High if mobile app exposes the route for digital subscription management.
technicalRisk: Medium: portal can allow subscription changes outside App Store or Play Billing from mobile context.
recommendedFix: Add server-side web-only guard and remove mobile portal entry points; use native store management links for store subscriptions.
testsToAdd: API tests that iOS and Android client headers are rejected for portal creation.

evidence: Applying App Store or Play Store state upserts the one user subscription row but does not clear stale Stripe customer, subscription, or price identifiers.
policyRisk: High: duplicate billing sources and incorrect manage links can confuse users and reviewers.
technicalRisk: Critical: later Stripe webhooks or reconciliation can overwrite current store-managed entitlement, and store purchases can coexist with old Stripe identifiers.
recommendedFix: Introduce a provider ledger plus derived entitlement, or explicitly archive/clear stale provider fields and enforce one active billing source with source priority.
testsToAdd: Cross-source tests: Stripe-to-IAP switch, IAP-to-Stripe attempt, stale Stripe webhook after IAP purchase, and duplicate active source prevention.

evidence: Stripe checkout blocks some Stripe states but does not clearly block users who already have an active App Store or Play Store subscription.
policyRisk: Medium: users may be offered web billing while already subscribed through a store.
technicalRisk: High: duplicate subscriptions are possible and source-of-truth behavior is ambiguous.
recommendedFix: Block Stripe checkout when current active entitlement is store-managed, or require an explicit migration/cancellation flow.
testsToAdd: Tests that store-managed users cannot start Stripe checkout unless migration rules allow it.

evidence: Google acknowledgement is attempted after active verification, but acknowledgement failure is logged and entitlement can still be returned as active.
policyRisk: Medium: unacknowledged purchases can be refunded by Google and create review/support issues.
technicalRisk: High: user may receive durable access for a purchase that later auto-refunds because acknowledgement failed.
recommendedFix: Make ack failure fail closed to pending entitlement or persist a durable retry job before granting durable access.
testsToAdd: Tests for ack success, ack failure retry, and entitlement state while ack is pending.

evidence: Stripe reconciliation exists, and admin UI refers to mobile billing revalidation, but no App Store or Play Store subscription revalidation cron route was found.
policyRisk: Medium: missed store notifications can leave stale entitlements visible in review and production.
technicalRisk: High: expired, refunded, or revoked store subscriptions can keep access until the user restores or another webhook arrives.
recommendedFix: Add scheduled Apple and Google reconciliation that refreshes stale store-managed subscriptions using stored transaction IDs and purchase tokens.
testsToAdd: Cron tests for Apple expiration, Play expiration, refund/revoke, stale validation, and retry behavior.

evidence: Apple revocation and refund signals are generally normalized to CANCELED rather than a distinct REFUNDED or revoked state.
policyRisk: Low to medium: support and subscription status can be unclear for users and reviewers.
technicalRisk: Medium: accounting, support, and entitlement audits lose refund/revoke semantics.
recommendedFix: Preserve refund/revoke notification type and revocation reason in normalized state and subscription audit fields.
testsToAdd: Tests for App Store REFUND, REVOKE, expiration, grace, and billing retry mapping.

area: Refund and voided purchase state mapping
evidence: Voided purchase notifications set subscription status to CANCELED and do not retain refund type/order details in normalized entitlement.
policyRisk: Low to medium: support and subscription status can be unclear for users and reviewers.
technicalRisk: Medium: refund/revoke analytics and entitlement audits lose event specificity.
recommendedFix: Persist voided purchase details and map refund/revoke states distinctly from voluntary cancellation.
testsToAdd: Tests for Play voided purchase notification and refunded subscription entitlement removal.

evidence: Mobile product IDs are exposed from runtime config names, but source cannot verify that App Store Connect and Play Console products exist, are active, or match monthly/yearly plan intent.
policyRisk: High: store review and purchase flow fail if products are missing, inactive, or mismatched.
technicalRisk: High: client can show unavailable products or map purchases to wrong plan/interval.
recommendedFix: Manually verify StoreKit product IDs, Play product IDs, subscription group, base plans, offers, prices, and runtime config before enabling mobile purchase.
testsToAdd: Config tests for required product IDs in mobile-enabled environments; sandbox product fetch tests for both stores.

evidence: RTDN route supports OIDC verification and package validation, but Pub/Sub topic, audience, expected service account, and Play Console RTDN setup are external to source.
policyRisk: High if production RTDN is absent or unverifiable during review/launch.
technicalRisk: High: renewals, cancellations, refunds, and grace-period changes can be missed.
recommendedFix: Verify Play Console RTDN, Pub/Sub push authentication, audience, and expected service account settings in staging and production.
testsToAdd: End-to-end RTDN test notification and signed Pub/Sub delivery test.

evidence: App Store notification route verifies signed notifications, but App Store Server Notification URL and sandbox/production delivery setup are external to source.
policyRisk: High if App Store notifications are not configured before launch.
technicalRisk: High: renewals, refunds, grace, billing retry, and revocations can be missed.
recommendedFix: Configure and verify App Store Server Notifications for sandbox and production environments.
testsToAdd: End-to-end App Store server notification tests for TEST, DID_RENEW, EXPIRED, REFUND, and REVOKE.

evidence: Source shows app identifiers and purchase code but cannot verify review notes, active IAP products, subscription declarations, or test account instructions.
policyRisk: High: store review can reject apps with login or subscription flows that reviewers cannot test.
technicalRisk: Medium: reviewers may see incomplete paywall or unavailable products.
recommendedFix: Prepare review notes, test account, sandbox instructions, product screenshots, and subscription disclosures; hide purchase UI until ready.
testsToAdd: Manual pre-submit checklist and scripted smoke test using reviewer account.

evidence: Public privacy page exists, but App Store privacy nutrition labels and Play Data Safety answers are not source-verifiable and must match account, address, document, analytics, notification, and payment data use.
policyRisk: Medium: inaccurate privacy declarations can block review or create compliance exposure.
technicalRisk: Medium: user data processing disclosures can drift from actual implementation.
recommendedFix: Complete and periodically audit App Privacy and Play Data Safety declarations against actual app behavior and processors.
testsToAdd: Store metadata review checklist and privacy declaration regression checklist.

evidence: No source-verifiable App Review or Play Review test credentials, sandbox testers, or reviewer purchase instructions were found.
policyRisk: High: login-required apps can be rejected if reviewers cannot access premium and purchase flows.
technicalRisk: Medium: review cannot validate restore, purchase, or existing subscriber paths.
recommendedFix: Create reviewer account, sandbox testers, and review notes explaining free/login, IAP, restore, and existing subscriber scenarios.
testsToAdd: Manual store-review dry run with reviewer account and sandbox purchase.

evidence: Purchase calls do not pass App Store app account token or Google obfuscated account/profile identifiers, and the database does not enforce purchaseToken uniqueness.
policyRisk: Low to medium: cross-account restore disputes are harder to prove.
technicalRisk: Medium: token replay and account binding rely mostly on backend transaction ownership checks after purchase.
recommendedFix: Pass stable hashed account identifiers supported by StoreKit/Play Billing and add database uniqueness or durable ownership checks for Google purchase tokens.
testsToAdd: Cross-account purchase and restore tests with two users and the same store account.

evidence: Unowned App Store notification logging can include full original transaction identifiers.
policyRisk: Low: not a payment policy blocker but long-lived purchase identifiers should be minimized in logs.
technicalRisk: Low: logs can expose purchase identifiers to broader internal audiences.
recommendedFix: Hash or partially redact transaction identifiers in logs and security events.
testsToAdd: Logging tests or static checks for token/transaction redaction.
